description: Legal deed. Dated: 4757 AM (996–97 CE). Ghulayb ha-Levi b. Shelomo, known as Salmān al-Tabīb, receives a loan from his brother Yiṣḥaq b. Salāma al-Ṭabīb (Goitein: Salāma ha-Levi b. Salman [!]), for which he mortgages his inheritance from their father, a quarter of the courtyard in the Roman fortress of Fusṭāṭ, in the alley of the Jerusalmites' synagogue. The loan of seven 'good dinars' is for two years, starting Av (1)308 Sel. The date of the deed is torn but the year 4757 AM is visible, and fits the Seleucid date mentioned regarding the loan. This is the earliest dated mention of the Shāmī synagogue (the one later known as the Ben ʿEzra). Join: Moshe Yagur. (Information in part from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מסמך משפטי בעברית. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
